prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins in a kitchen setting. A woman stands near an open oven, wearing an apron and blue rubber gloves. The oven door is open, and cleaning items appear on or near the floor in front of it. The kitchen has patterned walls, a window, and domestic furnishings.

The next frame cuts to a bright green product graphic. Large red lettering reads “Mr. Muscle,” with “NEW” in a yellow burst shape and “Oven Cleaner” underneath. This presents the advertised product clearly.

The sequence then moves to close-up views inside an oven. A blue-gloved hand uses a yellow cleaning pad or sponge on dirty oven surfaces. Overlaid white text reads “Minutes later” and, in one frame, “cheese,” positioned near a brownish stain. Another close-up shows the gloved hand cleaning along an oven surface with residue; the text “Minutes later” appears again, and the word “fat” is shown near the bottom. These frames visually demonstrate cleaning of different kinds of oven grime.

The commercial then cuts to a woman holding a green aerosol-style can of Mr. Muscle Oven Cleaner. She appears to be examining or presenting the product. Other people are visible behind her, but their exact setting and roles are unclear from the sampled frame.

The final frames show a product pack shot against a black background. A green can of Mr. Muscle Oven Cleaner stands on the left. Large white text to the right reads “New!!” The label on the can includes the Mr. Muscle branding and additional circular text indicating use “For Daytime” and “For Overnight.” The last frame holds essentially the same composition, reinforcing the product and the “New!!” message.
